title: allium viridulum
intro: Allium viridulum, also known as wild garlic or ransoms, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Allium genus and is native to North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. It is used to alleviate various health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, thanks to its expectorant and decongestant properties. Allium viridulum can be prepared as a tea, syrup, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies.
